title: Women's Mental Health Mapping and its Association with Domestic Violence in 'New Normal' Phase: Perspectives from Uttar Dinajpur District, India
author: Roy, Arabinda
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Domestic Violence (DV) is very critical, sensitive and socially relevant gender issues across all classes, castes, religions and community which includes all kind of violence against women and it is a serious public health problem across the world. The present research work attempts to access the inter-block inequality of domestic violence, mental health status of women and the association between them in Uttar Dinajpur, a district of West Bengal state, India. The study adopted both quantitative and qualitative techniques and is basically based on primary data which have been collected from field practiced area with the help of structured interview schedule and in-depth interview (open ended) process. The sampling units were the married women respondents and to collect the data, random sampling method were used and the total sample size is 450 (0.30%). To analyze the data, scale equivalence method, various descriptive statistics, Chi-Square Test has been used and maps are prepared in QGIS V3.4 software platform. The study depicts that there is a significant association and disparity in the level of domestic violence and mental health status of women. Finally, the study recommends some simple policy directions for the betterment of women’s well-being.

keywords: Domestic Violence; Public Health Issue; Mental Health; New Normal; PTSD
